title: Define the runtime or shared journal model and snapshot linkage
summary: Defined the runtime/shared journal contract, local-versus-shared behavior, and coarse snapshot-to-journal linkage without making published snapshots depend on journal availability.

acceptance.label: Publish manifests may reference runtime journal ranges without making snapshots depend on journal availability
Retention and compaction rules preserve high-resolution audit where desired without pushing that payload back into Git
Runtime or shared journal is the canonical home for fine-grained operational history between publish boundaries
